# Wann-O-Meter

> A structured, machine-readable calendar of "when is X" answers for Germany
> and beyond: public holidays, optimal vacation windows (bridge days), a
> seasonal produce calendar, and curated civic-data topic pages (elections,
> astronomical events, ...). Every entry has a canonical page, a JSON
> endpoint, and a subscribable ICS calendar feed - fetch the JSON, no
> scraping needed.

## Data catalog

- [Calendar catalog](https://wannometer.de/api/v1/calendar.json): every layer/subject on the site as `{id, group, label, url, feedUrl}` - the single index to discover everything below.
- [OpenAPI spec](https://wannometer.de/openapi.json): formal schema for every /api/v1/ and /feeds/ endpoint below - use this instead of guessing response shapes from prose.
- [Feiertage](https://wannometer.de/feiertage/): 221 pages. JSON: `https://wannometer.de/api/v1/feiertage/{slug}.json`. ICS (where dated events exist): `https://wannometer.de/feeds/feiertage/{slug}.ics`.
- [Saisonkalender](https://wannometer.de/saisonkalender/): 7 pages. JSON: `https://wannometer.de/api/v1/saisonkalender/{slug}.json`. ICS (where dated events exist): `https://wannometer.de/feeds/saisonkalender/{slug}.ics`.
- [Schulferien](https://wannometer.de/schulferien/): 16 pages. JSON: `https://wannometer.de/api/v1/schulferien/{slug}.json`. ICS (where dated events exist): `https://wannometer.de/feeds/schulferien/{slug}.ics`.
- [Urlaubsfenster](https://wannometer.de/urlaubsfenster/): 16 pages. JSON: `https://wannometer.de/api/v1/urlaubsfenster/{slug}.json`. ICS (where dated events exist): `https://wannometer.de/feeds/urlaubsfenster/{slug}.ics`.

## Notes for automated use

- All dates are ISO 8601 (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Every entry carries a `source`/`sources` block with the origin URL, license, and retrieval date - cite the original source, not this site, per that license.
- No authentication and no rate limit; please cache responses instead of re-fetching the same URL repeatedly.
- Full sitemap: https://wannometer.de/sitemap-index.xml
